subject: Change openjdk baseOS imgs to integration-*
commit_message:
Change openjdk baseOS imgs to integration-*

Benefits from switching over:
* minimal {java11,python} images maintained by integration team
* using currently "blessed by seccom" versions (if :latest tag used)
* should limit spread of legal issues across layers
* integration images will be the first to have automated compliance
  documentation
* should limit spread of base layers (contributing to deployment
  footprint - more base layers = more to download, more to store etc...)

Issue-ID: INT-1864
Signed-off-by: Alexander Mazuruk <a.mazuruk@samsung.com>
Change-Id: I9184e45b1d8cec294c04b462cf560f5b1c62d078

Patch Set 6:

> Patch Set 5:
> 
> > Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1
> > 
> > One final request.
> > Can we build the respective images and run them through the DR csit suite just to ensure no odd behaviour?
> > Thanks.
> 
> I'm only starting in ONAP, I assume version bump will trigger the csit suite? Did just that, if that is not what you meant please let me know what should I do, thanks!

No problem.
No need for a version bump.
Ideally we need to build the images locally. mvn clean package -P docker docker:build

Then run the dr csit suite against your local dr images.

Update the docker-compose.yaml to point to your local docker images
https://gerrit.onap.org/r/gitweb?p=integration/csit.git;a=blob;f=plans/dmaap-datarouter/ssl-dr-suite/docker-compose/docker-compose.yml;h=ed8a7c04a775c1982f336663c07bbcf26f466c3f;hb=d3a960e3773b81f8514b8b0dee70a8a5cfc54a28

Then from the csit project root: csit$ ./run-csit.sh plans/dmaap-datarouter/ssl-dr-suite/

ideally we should extend the gating to the project as we are doing today with clamp or SO (we build the docker from the patch then redeploy an ONAP with these dockers)
will the CSIT tests enough as non regression?
it would make sense to give at least a try to a manual check
